_comment: Queue of blog topics for the drafting agent. The agent pops the next topic where used=false, generates a draft, and marks it used=true. Add new topics by appending to the end of the array. Order matters — top of the unused list gets drafted first.

_format.title: Working title — the agent may refine the final title to fit 50–60 chars and the target keyword.
_format.targetKeyword: The single long-tail query this post is meant to rank for. Use Google autocomplete or a free tool to validate it.
_format.angle: One sentence describing the unique take. Helps prevent generic posts.
_format.internalLinks: Optional list of other prepfinity.co URLs this post should link to.
_format.used: true once the agent has drafted this topic. Don't manually set true; the script updates it.

topics.title: TCS technical interview process step by step
topics.angle: Walk through every round of the TCS NQT and Smart Hiring tracks with specifics that actually appear in 2026 (not 2018 copies).

topics.title: How to crack a Razorpay backend interview
topics.angle: What Razorpay actually asks (idempotency, payment flows, distributed transactions) — not generic backend questions.

topics.title: Service-based vs product-based company interviews compared
topics.targetKeyword: service based vs product based interview
topics.angle: What changes round-by-round: question depth, code expectations, follow-up style. With concrete TCS vs Razorpay examples.

topics.title: Top DSA patterns for SDE-1 fresher interviews
topics.angle: Pattern-based prep beats topic-by-topic prep. Cover the 8 patterns that account for most interview questions, with example problems.

topics.title: How to answer 'tell me about yourself' in tech interviews
topics.targetKeyword: tell me about yourself software engineer
topics.angle: A 4-part structure that works for freshers AND 5-year experienced. With three example answers at different experience levels.
topics.note: Covered by a hand-written post; skipped by the agent to avoid keyword cannibalization.

topics.title: System design interview prep for 2-5 years experience
topics.angle: What FAANG-India and Razorpay-tier startups actually expect at this level (vs senior expectations). Concrete examples.

topics.title: How to prepare for an Infosys interview
topics.angle: Specifics of the Infosys process (HackerRank round, technical, HR) with what each round is actually checking for.

topics.title: Free AI tools for interview preparation in India
topics.angle: Honest review of what works and what doesn't (yes, including a fair take on PrepFinity vs LeetCode AI vs InterviewBit).

topics.title: Behavioural interview questions for software engineers
topics.angle: The 12 questions that come up in 80% of HR rounds at MAANG and Indian startups, with STAR-format answer templates.
topics.note: Covered by a hand-written post; skipped by the agent to avoid keyword cannibalization.

topics.title: How to negotiate salary at a service company in India
topics.angle: Bands, bench risk, joining bonus realities. Specific scripts for the HR round at TCS, Infosys, Wipro.

topics.title: Voice mock interview practice vs writing answers
topics.angle: Why writing answers feels productive but doesn't fix the freeze you get in a real verbal interview. With drills that fix it.

topics.title: Common mistakes in HR round interviews for freshers
topics.angle: The 10 specific things freshers say in HR rounds that immediately tank their candidacy, with what to say instead.

topics.title: How to prepare for a Google India SWE interview
topics.angle: Realistic 8-week prep plan for Google's L3/L4 process — what to skip, what to over-prepare, what to do in week 7.

topics.title: What to do in the 24 hours before a technical interview
topics.angle: Sleep, food, mental rehearsal, no last-minute studying. A concrete hour-by-hour checklist with reasoning for each item.

topics.title: Resume tips for Indian software engineers with 0-3 years experience
topics.angle: What recruiters at TCS, Razorpay, and Google actually skim for in the first 7 seconds. Specific examples of bad vs good bullets.
